stepAnswer: Not reasonable because with an even k, the nearest neighbors could split evenly between the two classes, creating a tie with no clear majority vote when classifying a new point.
stepTitle: c) Brandon suggests that Dagny should use a different k for her classifier like k $$=$$ $$4$$ or k $$=$$ $$8$$. Is Brandon’s suggestion reasonable?
choices: Reasonable because a larger k is always better as using more neighbors gives a more accurate classification regardless of whether k is odd or even.
Reasonable because if there is a tie with an even k, we can just randomly assign the new point to either class without any downside.
Not reasonable because even values of k cause the algorithm to only look at points from one class, causing bias toward a certain prediction.
Not reasonable because with an even k, the nearest neighbors could split evenly between the two classes, creating a tie with no clear majority vote when classifying a new point.
